info.name: LiftTheBull API
info.description:
Complete API collection for LiftTheBull microservices.

**Services:**
- Auth Service (authentication and JWT tokens)
- [Add more services here as you build them]

**How to use:**
1. Import this collection
2. Import an environment file (staging or production)
3. Select the environment in the top-right dropdown
4. Start with Auth endpoints to get tokens
5. Tokens are automatically saved and used in other requests

**Authentication:**
Most endpoints require authentication. After logging in, the `accessToken` is automatically:
- Saved to environment variable
- Added to Authorization header in requests
- Refreshed when needed

item.name: Auth Service

exec: // Save tokens to environment variables for use in ALL other requests
console.log('✓ Access token saved (expires in 15 min)');
console.log('✓ Refresh token saved (expires in 30 days)');

request.description:
Creates a new user account and returns JWT tokens.

**Request Body:**
- `emailAddress` (string, required): User's email address
- `password` (string, required): User's password

**Response (201 Created):**
- `userId` (string): Generated unique user ID
- `emailAddress` (string): User's email
- `accessToken` (string): JWT access token (15 min expiry)
- `refreshToken` (string): JWT refresh token (30 day expiry)
- `accessTokenExpiresIn` (number): Access token expiration in seconds (900)
- `refreshTokenExpiresIn` (number): Refresh token expiration in seconds (2592000)

**What Happens:**
- User account is created in database
- User properties record is created
- Password is hashed with SHA256 + pepper
- JWT tokens are generated
- Welcome email is sent asynchronously

**Automatic Actions:**
- Saves `accessToken` for use in protected endpoints
- Saves `refreshToken` for token refresh
- Saves `userId` and `userEmail` for reference
- Welcome email sent to provided email address

exec: // Save tokens to environment variables for use in ALL other requests
console.log('✓ Access token saved (expires in 15 min)');
console.log('✓ Refresh token saved (expires in 30 days)');

request.description:
Authenticates an existing user and returns JWT tokens.

**Request Body:**
- `emailAddress` (string, required): User's email address
- `password` (string, required): User's password

**Response (200 OK):**
- `userId` (string): User's unique ID
- `emailAddress` (string): User's email
- `accessToken` (string): JWT access token (15 min expiry)
- `refreshToken` (string): JWT refresh token (30 day expiry)
- `accessTokenExpiresIn` (number): Access token expiration in seconds (900)
- `refreshTokenExpiresIn` (number): Refresh token expiration in seconds (2592000)

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid email or password

**Automatic Actions:**
- Saves `accessToken` for use in protected endpoints
- Saves `refreshToken` for token refresh
- Saves `userId` and `userEmail` for reference

exec: console.log('✓ New token saved (expires in 15 min)');
console.log('✓ You can now use protected endpoints again');

request.description:
Refreshes an expired access token using a valid refresh token.

**Request Body:**
- `refreshToken` (string, required): Valid JWT refresh token from login

**Response (200 OK):**
- `userId` (string): User's unique ID
- `accessToken` (string): New JWT access token (15 min expiry)
- `accessTokenExpiresIn` (number): Access token expiration in seconds (900)
- `refreshTokenExpiresIn` (number): Refresh token expiration in seconds (2592000)

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid, expired, or revoked refresh token

**When to use:**
- When access token expires (after 15 minutes)
- Before making API calls if token might be expired
- To maintain user session without re-login

**Automatic Actions:**
- Saves new `accessToken` to environment
- Token is immediately available for all protected endpoints

exec: // Clear tokens from environment after successful logout
console.log('✓ Refresh token cleared from environment');
console.log('✓ Access token still valid until expiration (15 min)');
console.log('⚠ Note: You will not be able to refresh your access token');

request.description:
Logs out the current user by removing their refresh token from the database.

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- Token is validated by Lambda Authorizer before processing

**Request:**
- No request body needed
- User ID is extracted from the access token

**Response (200 OK):**
- `message`: Confirmation message

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token
- Missing Authorization header

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

**What happens:**
- Refresh token is removed from database
- User cannot refresh access token anymore
- Current access token remains valid until expiration (15 min)
- User must login again to get new tokens

**Automatic Actions:**
- Clears `refreshToken` from environment
- Logs confirmation to console

exec: // Always returns success for security (doesn't reveal if email exists)
console.log('✓ If account exists, reset code has been sent to email');
console.log('⚠ Check email for 6-digit code (expires in 1 hour)');
console.log('⚠ Next: Use "Confirm Password Reset" with the code');

request.description:
Initiates password reset by sending a 6-digit code to the user's email.

**Request Body:**
- `emailAddress` (string, required): Email address to send reset code to

**Response (200 OK - Always):**
- `message`: "If an account exists for this email, a reset code has been sent"

**Security Features:**
- Always returns success (doesn't reveal if email exists)
- 6-digit random code generated
- Code expires in 1 hour
- Rate limited to 3 attempts per hour per user
- Email sent asynchronously via SES

**What happens:**
1. Validates email format
2. Looks up user by email (silently fails if not found)
3. Generates 6-digit code
4. Stores code in database with 1-hour TTL
5. Sends email with code to user
6. Returns success regardless of outcome

**Next Steps:**
1. Check email for 6-digit code
2. Use "Confirm Password Reset" endpoint with code
3. Code expires in 1 hour

Confirms password reset by validating the code and updating the password.

**Request Body:**
- `emailAddress` (string, required): User's email address
- `code` (string, required): 6-digit code from email
- `newPassword` (string, required): New password to set

**Response (200 OK):**
- `message`: "Password reset successfully"

**Error (400 Bad Request):**
- Missing required fields
- Invalid email format
- Code is not 6 digits

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired reset code
- Code doesn't match stored code
- User not found

**What happens:**
1. Validates email format and code format
2. Looks up user by email
3. Verifies reset code matches and hasn't expired
4. Hashes new password
5. Updates user's password in database
6. Deletes reset code (single-use)
7. Updates lastModifiedDatetime

**Security Notes:**
- Code must be exactly 6 digits
- Code expires after 1 hour
- Code is deleted after successful use (single-use)
- Password is hashed with SHA256 + pepper before storage

**After Reset:**
- User can immediately login with new password
- Old password no longer works
- All existing sessions remain valid until tokens expire

exec: // Save tokens to environment variables for use in ALL other requests
console.log('✓ Access token saved (expires in 15 min)');
console.log('✓ Refresh token saved (expires in 30 days)');

request.description:
Sign in with Apple identity token. Creates a new account if user doesn't exist, or logs in existing user.

**Request Body:**
- `identityToken` (string, required): JWT identity token from Apple Sign In
- `email` (string, optional): User's email address from Apple
- `fullName` (string, optional): User's full name from Apple

**Response (200 OK / 201 Created):**
- `userId` (string): User's unique ID
- `emailAddress` (string): User's email
- `accessToken` (string): JWT access token (15 min expiry)
- `refreshToken` (string): JWT refresh token (30 day expiry)
- `accessTokenExpiresIn` (number): Access token expiration in seconds (900)
- `refreshTokenExpiresIn` (number): Refresh token expiration in seconds (2592000)

**Automatic Actions:**
- Saves `accessToken` for use in protected endpoints
- Saves `refreshToken` for token refresh
- Saves `userId` and `userEmail` for reference

item.description:
Authentication endpoints for user management and JWT token operations.

**Authentication Flow:**
1. Create User or Login → Get tokens
2. Use `accessToken` in Authorization header for protected endpoints
3. When token expires (15 min), use Refresh Token endpoint
4. Repeat step 2 with new token
5. Use Logout to invalidate refresh token

**Password Reset Flow:**
1. Initiate Password Reset → Get 6-digit code via email
2. Check email for code (expires in 1 hour)
3. Confirm Password Reset with code and new password
4. Login with new password

**Token Storage:**
All tokens are automatically saved to environment variables:
- `accessToken` - Used in Authorization headers
- `refreshToken` - Used to get new access tokens
- `userId` - Current user's ID
- `userEmail` - Current user's email

item.name: User Service

request.description:
Retrieves user properties for the authenticated user.

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Response (200 OK):**
- `userId` (string): User's unique ID
- `availableChangePlates` (array): List of available change plate weights (numbers)
- `bodyweight` (number, optional): User's bodyweight (may be absent if not set)
- `createdDatetime` (string): When user properties were created
- `lastModifiedDatetime` (string): When user properties were last modified

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token
- Missing Authorization header

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

**Error (404 Not Found):**
- User properties not found (shouldn't happen if user was created properly)

Updates user properties for the authenticated user. Partial updates supported - only include fields you want to change.

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Request Body (all fields optional, at least one required):**
- `bodyweight` (number or null): User's bodyweight. Send null to remove.
- `availableChangePlates` (array of numbers): List of available change plate weights

**Response (200 OK):**
- `userId` (string): User's unique ID
- `bodyweight` (number, optional): Current bodyweight if set
- `availableChangePlates` (array): Current list of change plates
- `createdDatetime` (string): When user properties were created (unchanged)
- `lastModifiedDatetime` (string): Updated to current datetime

**Error (400 Bad Request):**
- No fields provided
- Invalid field type (bodyweight must be number or null, availableChangePlates must be array of numbers)

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token
- Missing Authorization header

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

Submits user feedback for the authenticated user.

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Request Body:**
- `message` (string, required): Feedback message from the user

**Response (200 OK):**
- `message`: Confirmation that feedback was submitted

**Error (400 Bad Request):**
- Missing message field

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

Deletes the authenticated user's account and all associated data.

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Request:**
- No request body needed
- User ID is extracted from the access token

**Response (200 OK):**
- `message`: Confirmation that account was deleted

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

**What happens:**
- User account and all associated data are permanently deleted
- All tokens are invalidated
- This action is irreversible

item.description:
User service endpoints for managing user-specific properties.

**All endpoints require authentication** via JWT access token.

**User Properties:**
- `bodyweight`: User's bodyweight (number, optional)
- `availableChangePlates`: List of available change plate weights (numbers)
- `createdDatetime`: Timestamp when properties were created
- `lastModifiedDatetime`: Timestamp when properties were last updated

User properties are automatically created when a user account is created with default values (availableChangePlates: []).

item.name: Checkin Service

request.description:
Creates or updates exercise check-ins for the authenticated user (upsert with batch support).

**Upsert Behavior:**
- **New items**: Creates with all fields including `createdDatetime`
- **Existing items**: Updates mutable fields (`name`, `isCustom`, `loadType`, `notes`), preserves `createdDatetime` and `createdTimezone`
- Ownership is verified via userId from JWT token (partition key)

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "exercises": [
    {
      "exerciseItemId": "uuid-string",
      "name": "Exercise name",
      "isCustom": true/false,
      "loadType": "Barbell" | "Bodyweight + Single Load" | "Single Load",
      "createdTimezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
      "notes": "Optional notes"
    }
  ]
}
```

**Exercise Fields:**
- `exerciseItemId` (string, required): Unique ID for this exercise (UUID from frontend)
- `name` (string, required): Exercise name
- `isCustom` (boolean, required): Whether this is a custom exercise
- `loadType` (string, required): Type of load - must be one of:
  - "Barbell"
  - "Bodyweight + Single Load"
  - "Single Load"
- `createdTimezone` (string, required): Timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los_Angeles")
- `notes` (string, optional): Optional notes about the exercise

**Response (200 OK for updates, 201 Created for new items):**
```json
{
  "exercises": [
    {
      "userId": "user-uuid",
      "exerciseItemId": "exercise-uuid",
      "name": "Bench Press",
      "isCustom": false,
      "loadType": "Barbell",
      "createdTimezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
      "notes": "Felt strong today",
      "createdDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z",
      "lastModifiedDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z"
    }
  ],
  "created": 1,
  "updated": 0
}
```

**Error (400 Bad Request):**
- Missing required fields
- Invalid loadType value
- Empty exercises array
- Validation errors (returns array of specific errors)

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

Retrieves all non-deleted exercise check-ins for the authenticated user.

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "exercises": [
    {
      "userId": "user-uuid",
      "exerciseItemId": "exercise-uuid-1",
      "name": "Bench Press",
      "isCustom": false,
      "loadType": "Barbell",
      "createdTimezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
      "notes": "Felt strong today",
      "createdDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z",
      "lastModifiedDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z"
    },
    {
      "userId": "user-uuid",
      "exerciseItemId": "exercise-uuid-2",
      "name": "Squats",
      "isCustom": false,
      "loadType": "Barbell",
      "createdTimezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
      "createdDatetime": "2026-01-27T11:00:00.000Z",
      "lastModifiedDatetime": "2026-01-27T11:00:00.000Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

**What it returns:**
- Only non-deleted exercises (where deleted != true)
- All exercises for the authenticated user
- Sorted by exerciseItemId (DynamoDB sort key)

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

Soft deletes one or more exercise check-ins by setting deleted=true (batch support).

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "exerciseItemIds": ["uuid-1", "uuid-2", ...]
}
```

**Response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "message": "Deleted 2 exercise(s)",
  "deletedExercises": [
    {
      "userId": "user-uuid",
      "exerciseItemId": "exercise-uuid",
      "name": "Bench Press",
      "isCustom": false,
      "loadType": "Barbell",
      "createdTimezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
      "notes": "Felt strong today",
      "createdDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z",
      "lastModifiedDatetime": "2026-01-27T15:00:00.000Z",
      "deleted": true
    }
  ],
  "notFoundIds": ["uuid-that-didnt-exist"]
}
```

**What happens:**
- Sets `deleted` attribute to `true` for each exercise
- Updates `lastModifiedDatetime` to current time
- Exercises no longer appear in GET /checkin/exercises results
- Exercise data is preserved in database (soft delete)
- Returns list of successfully deleted exercises and any IDs that weren't found

**Error (400 Bad Request):**
- Missing exerciseItemIds field
- Empty exerciseItemIds array
- exerciseItemIds is not an array

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

Creates one or more lift sets for the authenticated user (batch support).

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "liftSets": [
    {
      "liftSetId": "uuid-string",
      "exerciseId": "uuid-of-exercise",
      "reps": 10,
      "weight": 135.5,
      "createdTimezone": "America/Los_Angeles"
    }
  ]
}
```

**Lift Set Fields:**
- `liftSetId` (string, required): Unique ID for this lift set (UUID from frontend)
- `exerciseId` (string, required): ID of the associated exercise
- `reps` (integer, required): Number of repetitions (non-negative)
- `weight` (number, required): Weight used (decimal allowed)
- `createdTimezone` (string, required): Timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los_Angeles")

**Response (201 Created):**
```json
{
  "liftSets": [
    {
      "userId": "user-uuid",
      "liftSetId": "lift-set-uuid",
      "exerciseId": "exercise-uuid",
      "reps": 10,
      "weight": 135.5,
      "createdTimezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
      "createdDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z",
      "lastModifiedDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z"
    }
  ],
  "created": 1
}
```

**Error (400 Bad Request):**
- Missing required fields
- reps is not a non-negative integer
- weight is not a number
- Empty liftSets array

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

query.description: Number of items per page (default 100, max 500)
Token from previous response for next page

request.description:
Retrieves paginated lift sets for the authenticated user, ordered by most recent first.

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Query Parameters:**
- `limit` (optional): Number of items per page (default: 100, max: 500)
- `pageToken` (optional): Token from previous response for pagination

**Response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "liftSets": [
    {
      "userId": "user-uuid",
      "liftSetId": "lift-set-uuid",
      "exerciseId": "exercise-uuid",
      "reps": 10,
      "weight": 135.5,
      "createdTimezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
      "createdDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z",
      "lastModifiedDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z"
    }
  ],
  "count": 100,
  "hasMore": true,
  "nextPageToken": "base64-encoded-token"
}
```

**Pagination:**
- Results ordered by `createdDatetime` descending (most recent first)
- Use `nextPageToken` in subsequent requests to get next page
- `hasMore` indicates if more pages are available
- Filters out soft-deleted items

**Sync Strategy:**
1. Call without pageToken to get most recent items
2. If `hasMore` is true, call again with `pageToken=nextPageToken`
3. Repeat until `hasMore` is false

**Error (400 Bad Request):**
- Invalid pageToken

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

Soft deletes one or more lift sets by setting deleted=true (batch support).

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "liftSetIds": ["uuid-1", "uuid-2", ...]
}
```

**Response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "message": "Deleted 2 lift set(s)",
  "deletedLiftSets": [
    {
      "userId": "user-uuid",
      "liftSetId": "lift-set-uuid",
      "exerciseId": "exercise-uuid",
      "reps": 10,
      "weight": 135.5,
      "createdTimezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
      "createdDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z",
      "lastModifiedDatetime": "2026-01-27T15:00:00.000Z",
      "deleted": true
    }
  ],
  "notFoundIds": ["uuid-that-didnt-exist"]
}
```

**What happens:**
- Sets `deleted` attribute to `true` for each lift set
- Updates `lastModifiedDatetime` to current time
- Lift sets no longer appear in GET /checkin/lift-sets results
- Data is preserved in database (soft delete)
- Returns list of successfully deleted lift sets and any IDs not found

**Error (400 Bad Request):**
- Missing liftSetIds field
- Empty liftSetIds array
- liftSetIds is not an array

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

Creates one or more estimated 1RM records for the authenticated user (batch support).

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "estimated1RMs": [
    {
      "estimated1RMId": "uuid-string",
      "liftSetId": "uuid-of-lift-set",
      "exerciseId": "uuid-of-exercise",
      "value": 225.5,
      "createdTimezone": "America/Los_Angeles"
    }
  ]
}
```

**Estimated 1RM Fields:**
- `estimated1RMId` (string, required): Unique ID for this E1RM record (UUID from frontend)
- `liftSetId` (string, required): ID of the lift set that achieved this E1RM
- `exerciseId` (string, required): ID of the associated exercise
- `value` (number, required): The estimated 1RM value (decimal allowed)
- `createdTimezone` (string, required): Timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los_Angeles")

**Response (201 Created):**
```json
{
  "estimated1RMs": [
    {
      "userId": "user-uuid",
      "liftSetId": "lift-set-uuid",
      "estimated1RMId": "e1rm-uuid",
      "exerciseId": "exercise-uuid",
      "value": 225.5,
      "createdTimezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
      "createdDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z",
      "lastModifiedDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z"
    }
  ],
  "created": 1
}
```

**Error (400 Bad Request):**
- Missing required fields
- value is not a number
- Empty estimated1RMs array

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

query.description: Number of items per page (default 100, max 500)
Token from previous response for next page

request.description:
Retrieves paginated estimated 1RM records for the authenticated user, ordered by most recent first.

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Query Parameters:**
- `limit` (optional): Number of items per page (default: 100, max: 500)
- `pageToken` (optional): Token from previous response for pagination

**Response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "estimated1RMs": [
    {
      "userId": "user-uuid",
      "liftSetId": "lift-set-uuid",
      "estimated1RMId": "e1rm-uuid",
      "exerciseId": "exercise-uuid",
      "value": 225.5,
      "createdTimezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
      "createdDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z",
      "lastModifiedDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z"
    }
  ],
  "count": 100,
  "hasMore": true,
  "nextPageToken": "base64-encoded-token"
}
```

**Pagination:**
- Results ordered by `createdDatetime` descending (most recent first)
- Use `nextPageToken` in subsequent requests to get next page
- `hasMore` indicates if more pages are available
- Filters out soft-deleted items

**Error (400 Bad Request):**
- Invalid pageToken

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

Soft deletes one or more estimated 1RM records by setting deleted=true (batch support).

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "liftSetIds": ["lift-set-uuid-1", "lift-set-uuid-2", ...]
}
```

Note: We use `liftSetIds` because `liftSetId` is the sort key for this table.

**Response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "message": "Deleted 2 estimated 1RM record(s)",
  "deletedEstimated1RMs": [
    {
      "userId": "user-uuid",
      "liftSetId": "lift-set-uuid",
      "estimated1RMId": "e1rm-uuid",
      "exerciseId": "exercise-uuid",
      "value": 225.5,
      "createdTimezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
      "createdDatetime": "2026-01-27T10:30:00.000Z",
      "lastModifiedDatetime": "2026-01-27T15:00:00.000Z",
      "deleted": true
    }
  ],
  "notFoundIds": ["uuid-that-didnt-exist"]
}
```

**What happens:**
- Sets `deleted` attribute to `true` for each estimated 1RM
- Updates `lastModifiedDatetime` to current time
- Records no longer appear in GET /checkin/estimated-1rm results
- Data is preserved in database (soft delete)
- Returns list of successfully deleted records and any IDs not found

**Error (400 Bad Request):**
- Missing liftSetIds field
- Empty liftSetIds array
- liftSetIds is not an array

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

item.description:
Checkin service endpoints for managing exercise check-ins, lift sets, and estimated 1RM records.

**All endpoints require authentication** via JWT access token and API key.

**Exercise Endpoints (/checkin/exercises):**
- POST: Upsert exercises (batch)
- GET: Get all non-deleted exercises
- DELETE: Soft delete exercises (batch)

**Lift Set Endpoints (/checkin/lift-sets):**
- POST: Create lift sets (batch)
- GET: Get paginated lift sets (most recent first)
- DELETE: Soft delete lift sets (batch)

**Estimated 1RM Endpoints (/checkin/estimated-1rm):**
- POST: Create estimated 1RM records (batch)
- GET: Get paginated estimated 1RM records (most recent first)
- DELETE: Soft delete estimated 1RM records (batch)

**Exercise Fields:**
- `exerciseItemId`: Unique identifier (UUID from frontend)
- `name`: Exercise name
- `isCustom`: Whether this is a custom exercise (true/false)
- `loadType`: Type of load - "Barbell", "Bodyweight + Single Load", or "Single Load"
- `createdTimezone`: Timezone identifier
- `notes`: Optional notes
- `createdDatetime`: When exercise was created (ISO 8601)
- `lastModifiedDatetime`: When exercise was last modified (ISO 8601)
- `deleted`: Soft delete flag (only present when true)

**Lift Set Fields:**
- `liftSetId`: Unique identifier (UUID from frontend)
- `exerciseId`: Associated exercise ID
- `reps`: Number of repetitions (integer)
- `weight`: Weight used (decimal)
- `createdTimezone`: Timezone identifier
- `createdDatetime`: When lift set was created (ISO 8601)
- `lastModifiedDatetime`: When lift set was last modified (ISO 8601)
- `deleted`: Soft delete flag

**Estimated 1RM Fields:**
- `estimated1RMId`: Unique identifier (UUID from frontend)
- `liftSetId`: ID of the lift set that achieved this E1RM (also the sort key)
- `exerciseId`: Associated exercise ID
- `value`: The estimated 1RM value (decimal)
- `createdTimezone`: Timezone identifier
- `createdDatetime`: When E1RM was created (ISO 8601)
- `lastModifiedDatetime`: When E1RM was last modified (ISO 8601)
- `deleted`: Soft delete flag

**Batch Operations:**
- All POST endpoints accept arrays for batch create
- All DELETE endpoints accept arrays for batch soft delete

**Soft Delete:**
Deleted items are marked with `deleted=true` and filtered out from GET requests. Data is preserved in the database.

item.name: Entitlements Service

request.description:
Returns the current account subscription status for the authenticated user.

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "accountStatus": "free",
  "expirationUtc": null
}
```

Or for premium users:
```json
{
  "accountStatus": "premium",
  "expirationUtc": "2025-12-01T09:32:55.000"
}
```

**Response Fields:**
- `accountStatus` (string): Either "free" or "premium"
- `expirationUtc` (string|null): ISO 8601 expiration date for premium, null for free

**How it works:**
- Queries entitlement grants table for active subscriptions
- Returns "premium" if any grant has endUtc > current time
- Returns the latest expiration date among active grants

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

Process Apple App Store transactions and create entitlement grants.

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- Requires valid access token in Authorization header
- User ID is extracted from the JWT token

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "apple": {
    "originalTransactionIds": ["1000000123456789"]
  }
}
```

**Request Fields:**
- `apple.originalTransactionIds` (string[], required): Array of Apple original transaction IDs from StoreKit

**Response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "activeEntitlements": [
    {
      "userId": "user-uuid",
      "startUtc": "2025-11-01T09:32:55.000",
      "endUtc": "2025-12-01T09:32:55.000",
      "entitlementName": "premium",
      "paymentPlatformSource": "apple",
      "originalTransactionId": "1000000123456789",
      "productId": "com.app.premium.monthly",
      "createdDatetime": "2025-11-01T09:32:55.000Z",
      "lastModifiedDatetime": "2025-11-01T09:32:55.000Z"
    }
  ],
  "created": 2,
  "skipped": 1
}
```

**Response Fields:**
- `activeEntitlements` (array): List of currently active entitlement grants for the user
- `created` (number): Number of new entitlement grants created
- `skipped` (number): Number of grants skipped because they already existed

**How it works:**
1. Fetches transaction history from Apple using originalTransactionId
2. Creates entitlement grants for each subscription period
3. Uses conditional writes to avoid duplicates (userId + startUtc must be unique)
4. Returns all currently active entitlements

**When to call:**
- After a successful in-app purchase
- On app launch to sync entitlements
- After restoring purchases

**Important:**
- Use `originalTransactionId` from StoreKit (not the regular transactionId)
- This fetches the full transaction history including renewals

**Error (400 Bad Request):**
- Missing apple.originalTransactionIds
- Empty originalTransactionIds array

**Error (401 Unauthorized):**
- Invalid or expired access token

**Error (403 Forbidden):**
- Missing or invalid API key

Apple Server Notification V2 webhook endpoint.

**Authentication:**
- Requires API key in x-api-key header
- NO JWT authentication (Apple cannot authenticate with our tokens)

**Request Body:**
Apple sends a signed notification payload:
```json
{
  "signedPayload": "eyJhbGciOiJFUzI1NiIsIng1YyI6W..."
}
```

**Response (200 OK - Always):**
```json
{
  "message": "ok"
}
```

**Important:** This endpoint ALWAYS returns 200, even on errors. This prevents Apple from retrying notifications unnecessarily.

**How it works:**
1. Receives signed notification from Apple
2. Decodes and verifies the notification using Apple's certificates
3. Extracts `appAccountToken` from the transaction (this is the userId)
4. Verifies the user exists in our users table
5. Fetches transaction history using originalTransactionId
6. Creates entitlement grants for each subscription period

**Notification Types Handled:**
- SUBSCRIBED: New subscription
- DID_RENEW: Subscription renewed
- EXPIRED: Subscription expired
- DID_CHANGE_RENEWAL_STATUS: Auto-renewal toggled
- And others...

**iOS App Setup:**
When initiating purchases, set the `appAccountToken` to the user's userId:
```swift
let purchase = try await product.purchase(options: [
    .appAccountToken(UUID(uuidString: userId)!)
])
```

**App Store Connect Setup:**
1. Go to App Store Connect > Your App > App Information
2. Scroll to "App Store Server Notifications"
3. Set Production URL: `https://{api-id}.execute-api.{region}.amazonaws.com/production/entitlements/apple-notification`
4. Set Sandbox URL: `https://{api-id}.execute-api.{region}.amazonaws.com/staging/entitlements/apple-notification`
5. Note: You may need to include the API key as a query parameter or configure it separately

**Testing:**
This endpoint is typically called by Apple automatically. For testing:
1. Use Apple's Sandbox environment
2. Make test purchases with sandbox accounts
3. Monitor CloudWatch logs for notification processing

item.description:
Entitlements service endpoints for managing subscription entitlements via Apple App Store Server API.

**Endpoints:**
- GET /entitlements/status: Get current account status (free/premium)
- POST /entitlements: Process Apple transactions and sync entitlements
- POST /entitlements/apple-notification: Apple webhook (called by Apple)

**Entitlement Grant Fields:**
- `userId`: User's unique identifier
- `startUtc`: Subscription start date (ISO 8601)
- `endUtc`: Subscription end date (ISO 8601)
- `entitlementName`: Type of entitlement (e.g., "premium")
- `paymentPlatformSource`: Payment platform ("apple")
- `originalTransactionId`: Apple's original transaction ID
- `productId`: Apple's product ID
- `createdDatetime`: When grant was created
- `lastModifiedDatetime`: When grant was last modified

**Integration Flow:**
1. iOS app makes in-app purchase with `appAccountToken` set to userId
2. On purchase success, call POST /entitlements with originalTransactionId
3. Apple sends notifications to webhook for renewals/cancellations
4. Call GET /entitlements/status to check current subscription state

**Apple Credentials (SSM Parameters):**
Before using, configure these in AWS SSM Parameter Store:
- `/{project}/{env}/entitlements/apple-private-key`: .p8 file contents
- `/{project}/{env}/entitlements/apple-key-id`: Key ID from App Store Connect
- `/{project}/{env}/entitlements/apple-issuer-id`: Issuer ID
- `/{project}/{env}/entitlements/apple-bundle-id`: App bundle ID

Insights service endpoints for training insights, weekly narratives, and tier progression.

**All endpoints require authentication** via JWT access token and API key.

**Endpoints:**
- GET /insights/weekly: Get weekly training insights (premium-gated)
- GET /insights/starter: Get starter insight for new users
- POST /insights/tier-unlock: Trigger a tier unlock
- GET /insights/tier-unlocks: Get all tier unlocks
